leadText: A Durin Berry (Japanese: ドリのみ Dori Fruit) is a type of Berry introduced in Generation III.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Ingredient 1.2 Exchange 1.2.1 Generation III 1.2.2 Generation IV 1.2.3 Pokémon Dream World Effect [ edit | edit source ] Durin Berries cannot be used directly and only have a use as an ingredient, such as for Pokéblocks and Poffins . Ingredient [ edit | edit source ] Berry Blending R S E When blending with NPCs, Durin Berries will produce the following Pokéblocks : NPC NPCs' Berries Flavors ( 100 RPM ) Feel 1 NPC Bitterness 51, Sourness 12 45 2 NPCs Bitterness 51, Spiciness 12 37 3 NPCs Bitterness 51, Dryness 12 32 Blend Master Bitterness 51, Sourness 25 48 OR AS Durin Berries are green Berries that have a low chance of producing a Pokéblock +. Berry Crush FR LG E Durin Berries have a base Berry Powder of 250 and a difficulty of 160. With two players, this Berry will contribute 500 units of Berry Powder at 100% silkiness. Poffin Cooking D P Pt BD SP When used alone, Durin Berries will produce Poffins with Bitterness 19, Sourness 9 and smoothness 34 when cooked in 1 minute with no spills or burns. Ice Cream Scoop DW Flavor 1 Flavor 2 Flavor 3 Easy to scoop Stickiness Juice Shoppe X Y Durin Berries are green Berries in Group 1. Composting X Y Durin Berries are green Berries. Exchange [ edit | edit source ] Generation III [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Emerald , the player can exchange a Durin Berry for an HP Up if the Favor Lady asks for 'something pointy' in the Lilycove Pokémon Center . A Durin Berry can also be exchanged for an Iron if the Favor Lady asks for 'something wham-ish'. Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Diamond, Pearl , and Platinum , the player can exchange Durin Berries for accessories at the Pick a Peck of Colors Flower Shop . In Pokémon Diamond and Pearl, the player can exchange: 250 Durin Berries for a Mirror Ball In Pokémon Platinum, the player can exchange: 100 Durin Berries for a Fluffy Bed Pokémon Dream World [ edit | edit source ] In the Pokémon Dream World , the player could exchange Berries for Décor . The following Décor required Durin Berries. Catalogue Décor Price Plain Catalogue Rocking Chair 40 Durin Berries 40 Belue Berries Plain Catalogue Flower Bath Mat 20 Durin Berries 20 Watmel Berries Plain Catalogue House Plant 100 Durin Berries 60 Leppa Berries Plain Catalogue Poké Ball Rug 35 Durin Berries 35 Belue Berries 35 Watmel Berries Plain Catalogue Tree House 300 Durin Berries 300 Leppa Berries 300 Belue Berries Plain Catalogue Tropical Hut 300 Durin Berries 300 Leppa Berries 300 Watmel Berries Pokémon Doll Catalogue Togepi Doll 50 Durin Berries Pokémon Doll Catalogue Seviper Doll 50 Durin Berries Pokémon Doll Catalogue Emolga Doll 50 Durin Berries

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Durin Berries raise Snorlax's Strength, doubling the effect if the Berry is Snorlax's favorite. The base amount of Strength raised by the Berry is determined by the level of the helper Pokémon that gathered it.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text: Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Finite methods Repeatable methods R S E Route 123 ( Berry Master's wife ) Lilycove City ( Quiz Lady ) E Mossdeep Game Corner ( Dodrio Berry Picking with 5 players) E FR LG Pickup (any level ) Joyful Game Corner ( Dodrio Berry Picking with 5 players) Colo XD Trade D P Pt Amity Square (found by walking Pokémon , given by a man Pt ) Spin Trade (5 players) Pt HG SS Fuchsia City Spin Trade (5 players) PW Big Forest B W B2 W2 Transfer from Dream World DW * Icy Cave X Y Trade OR AS Route 123 ( Berry Masters's wife ) Hyper Rank Pokémon Contest Spectacular (given by fan) BD SP Amity Square (found by walking Pokémon )

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text: Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Durin Berries can be gathered by Grass-type helper Pokémon in the player's team. Like all Berries in the game, gathered Durin Berries do not go into the player's inventory and instead are immediately eaten by Snorlax.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description R S E P O K é block ingredient. Plant in loamy soil to grow Durin . FR LG Can be ground up into a powder as an ingredient for medicine. Colo XD An item brought over from a faraway place. D P Pt A Poffin ingredient. Bury it in soft soil to grow a Durin Plant. HG SS In the Sinnoh region, they like to make sweets known as Poffins with this Berry and feed them to their Pokémon. B W B2 W2 A Berry which is very rare in the Unova region. A maniac will buy it for a high price. X Y A Berry to be used in cooking. This Berry is very rare and hard to obtain in the Unova region. OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh LA Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your Cleverness , this Berry is rare in other regions. BD SP A Poffin ingredient. Bury it in soft soil to grow a Durin Berry Plant. S V Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance Cleverness . This Berry is rare in other regions. Tag information [ edit | edit source ] No. 34 Size: 11.0" (28.0 cm) Firmness: Hard Games Description R S E Bitter to even look at. It is so bitter, no one has ever eaten it as is. D P Pt BD SP This Berry is tremendously bitter. Just one bite is enough to instantly stop hiccups.

Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Finite methods Repeatable methods R S E Route 123 ( Berry Master's wife ) Lilycove City ( Quiz Lady ) E Mossdeep Game Corner ( Dodrio Berry Picking with 5 players) E FR LG Pickup (any level ) Joyful Game Corner ( Dodrio Berry Picking with 5 players) Colo XD Trade D P Pt Amity Square (found by walking Pokémon , given by a man Pt ) Spin Trade (5 players) Pt HG SS Fuchsia City Spin Trade (5 players) PW Big Forest B W B2 W2 Transfer from Dream World DW * Icy Cave X Y Trade OR AS Route 123 ( Berry Masters's wife ) Hyper Rank Pokémon Contest Spectacular (given by fan) BD SP Amity Square (found by walking Pokémon ) Growth [ edit | edit source ] Generation III Planted 0 hours Sprouted 18 hours Taller 36 hours Flowering 54 hours Berries 72 hours A Durin plant will yield 1-2 Berries. Generation IV Planted 0 hours Sprouted 15 hours Taller 30 hours Flowering 45 hours Berries 60 hours A Durin plant will yield 2-15 Berries. A growing Durin plant's soil dries by 8% every hour, fully drying out in 13 hours. Generation V Planted 0 hours Sprouted 22.5 hours Taller 45 hours Flowering 67.5 hours Berries 90 hours A Durin plant will yield ? Berries. Generation VI Pokémon X and Y Planted 0 hours Sprouted 4 hours Growing 8 hours Bigger 12 hours Budding 16 hours Bloom 20 hours Berries 24 hours A Durin plant will yield 3-15 Berries. During its growth, watering the plant adds 1 Berries to the final harvest, weeding it adds 1, and removing a pest adds 3. Durin plants attract Burmy as pests. Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire Planted 0 hours Sprouted 4 hours Taller 8 hours Flowering 12 hours Berries 16 hours A Durin plant will yield 3-15 Berries. Generation VII Pokémon Sun, Moon, Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon Planted 0 hours Sprouted 6 hours Taller 12 hours Flowering 20 hours Berries 24 hours A Durin plant will yield 4 Berries if Isle Aplenny is at Phase 1, 8 Berries if it is at Phase 2, and 12 Berries if it is at Phase 3. Generation VIII Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl Planted 0 hours Sprouted 15 hours Taller 30 hours Flowering 45 hours Berries 60 hours A Durin plant will yield 2-15 Berries.
